description: Learn why authorization is critical for web application security and how broken access control became the #1 vulnerability in OWASP's Top 10.
An overview of the major permission system approaches: RBAC, ABAC, and ReBAC, with guidance on when to use each.
Tour the Document Management System starter app and understand why it currently has no security - anyone can do anything.
Understand the core concepts of Role-Based Access Control: roles, permissions, and how they map together.
Create the role and permission type definitions in TypeScript that will be the foundation of our authorization system.
Build Express middleware that checks user roles before allowing access to protected routes.
Add role-based UI controls in React to show, hide, and disable elements based on user permissions.
See what happens when you only check permissions on the frontend - and why backend validation is essential.
Understand the limitations of pure RBAC and when you need to add attribute-based rules.
Learn the core concepts of ABAC: attributes, policies, and how to make context-aware permission decisions.
Implement ownership checks so editors can only edit documents they created.
Demo of ID enumeration attack and how to prevent users from accessing resources by guessing IDs.
Add organization-level isolation so users can only see and interact with their organization's data.
Demo of mass assignment vulnerability where users can modify fields they shouldn't have access to.
Implement field-level read permissions to hide sensitive data from unauthorized users.
Implement field-level write permissions to control which fields users can modify.
Learn how to combine multiple conditions into complex policies that handle real-world business rules.
Understand relationship-based access control and how it models permissions as a graph of relationships.
Implement document sharing with a relationship table and permission checks.
Handle complex nested relationships like organization membership implying document access.
Brief overview of Google Zanzibar and production ReBAC systems like SpiceDB and OpenFGA.
Introduction to CASL, a popular isomorphic authorization library for JavaScript/TypeScript.
Refactor our permission system to use CASL's defineAbility and ability checks.
Add TypeScript types to CASL for compile-time safety and IDE autocomplete.
Overview of Casbin's DSL-based approach to authorization with external policy files.
Compare CASL and Casbin to understand when to use each library.
Design the API for our custom permission library with a focus on simplicity and type safety.
Define the TypeScript types and interfaces for our permission library.
Implement the builder pattern for collecting permission rules.
Implement the can() and cannot() methods for checking permissions.
Implement condition matching logic using simple equality checks.
Implement field-level permission checking and filtering.
Generate Drizzle ORM where clauses from permission rules.
Create React components and hooks for frontend permission checks.
Put it all together and see our custom permission library in action.
Best practices for implementing permission checks in React applications.
Compare middleware-based vs service-layer permission checking approaches.
A subtle race condition in permission checking and how to prevent it.
See the complete permission flow from login to database query to UI rendering.
Understand when to use code-based permissions vs a UI-based permission editor.
Create a database schema to store permission rules that can be edited at runtime.
Build a simple visual interface for viewing and editing permission rules.
Create a modal form for adding and editing permission rules with dropdowns and checkboxes.
Load permission rules from the database and build abilities dynamically.
Best practices for permission editors including safety guardrails and audit logging.
A decision framework for choosing between RBAC, ABAC, and ReBAC based on your application's needs.
Performance optimization, audit logging, and other considerations for production permission systems.
Recommended tools, libraries, and learning resources for further exploration of permission systems.
